description:
You are helping an incident-response analyst who needs a quick look at all error events that happened on 15 May 2023.  
    Everything you need is already in the workstation’s home directory.

    1. Source log  
       • Full path: /home/user/logs/system.log  
       • Format: one entry per line →  
         [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] [LEVEL] Message text

    2. Your objective  
       • With a **single terminal command line** (pipelines are allowed),  
         – read /home/user/logs/system.log,  
         – select only the lines that contain the exact, case-sensitive token “[ERROR]”,  
         – *simultaneously*  
           ▫ print those lines to STDOUT **in the same order they appear in the file**, and  
           ▫ write them to a new file named /home/user/logs/incident_ERRORs_20230515.log.  

    3. Output expectations  
       • The command’s STDOUT must consist of **exactly** the two lines shown below, with no leading/trailing blank lines or extra characters:  
         [2023-05-15 10:05:42] [ERROR] Failed to mount /dev/sda1  
         [2023-05-15 10:12:55] [ERROR] Network unreachable  
       • The newly created file /home/user/logs/incident_ERRORs_20230515.log must contain those exact same two lines, newline-terminated.

    4. Verification notes (for you)  
       • Use whatever tooling you like (grep, awk, sed, etc.) but keep everything on a single shell command line.  
       • Do *not* manually open or edit /home/user/logs/system.log; the task is about using command-line text-processing.  
       • Avoid superfluous echo statements: the grader checks that STDOUT matches the two required log lines and nothing else.  
       • When you’re satisfied, simply run the command. The automated test will:  
         – look for /home/user/logs/incident_ERRORs_20230515.log,  
         – verify its contents, and  
         – compare your command’s captured STDOUT to the required lines.
truth:
Initial state:  
    • Directory /home/user/logs already exists with 755 permissions.  
    • File /home/user/logs/system.log exists with mode 644 and the following exact content (newline at end of each line):  
      [2023-05-15 10:03:17] [INFO] System booting  
      [2023-05-15 10:05:42] [ERROR] Failed to mount /dev/sda1  
      [2023-05-15 10:07:03] [WARNING] Low disk space  
      [2023-05-15 10:12:55] [ERROR] Network unreachable  
      [2023-05-15 10:15:00] [INFO] User login: alice  

    Expected artefacts after correct execution:  
    • File /home/user/logs/incident_ERRORs_20230515.log must exist, mode 644 (default umask ok), with content:  
      [2023-05-15 10:05:42] [ERROR] Failed to mount /dev/sda1  
      [2023-05-15 10:12:55] [ERROR] Network unreachable  
      (trailing newline after the second line acceptable)  

    • The agent’s last command STDOUT must exactly match the two lines above, order preserved, with a single newline separating them (and an optional trailing newline at the very end).

    The grader will:  
    1. Confirm that the output file exists.  
    2. diff the file’s contents against the two expected lines.  
    3. Compare captured STDOUT to the exact same expected lines.  
    Any deviation (extra lines, missing lines, wrong order, incorrect filename) will fail the test.
